goal: Fix invoice totals that round each line item instead of rounding once at the total
acceptance_criteria: Line items are summed at full precision and the total is rounded once to 2 decimals
Existing invoices with single line items are unchanged
A regression test reproduces the reported off-by-one-cent bug
constraints: No change to the public Invoice API shape
assumptions: All amounts are in minor units handled as Decimal, confirmed in billing/money.py
verification_plan: Add a failing unit test that reproduces the rounding bug

patterns_to_follow: Decimal quantize with ROUND_HALF_UP used in billing/money.py

minimality_decision.reason: Move the single quantize() call from per-line-item to the summed total; the summation and Decimal handling already exist.

rejected_lower_rungs.why_not: Totals are demonstrably wrong by one cent on multi-line invoices.
No dead code is responsible; the rounding location is the bug.
Reuse applies — the existing quantize helper is reused; the change is where it is called.

plan: Sum line items at full Decimal precision
Quantize the total once to 2 decimals with the existing ROUND_HALF_UP helper
Add a regression test for a 3-line invoice that previously mis-rounded

commands_run.evidence: Red-green: failed before fix (expected 10.00, got 10.01), passes after the rounding move
14 passed after fix, including new regression test
no issues found in 9 source files

open_risks: Banker's rounding vs ROUND_HALF_UP could differ for .005 cases; confirmed ROUND_HALF_UP matches existing money.py policy
review_findings: Fresh-context review: diff matches contract; rounding moved to total; regression test covers the reported case; no API change. Verdict: approve.
